description_long: This cowshed is an L-shaped, single-story structure with a dark, pitched roof that features both gable and hip elements. The exterior walls are clad in vertical wooden planks, giving it a rustic yet functional appearance. Multiple rectangular windows are evenly spaced along the longer wing, providing natural light to the interior, while the shorter wing has fewer windows. Two solid, dark-colored doors are visible, each centrally placed on the respective wings, likely serving as main entrances for livestock and personnel. The roof is made of a smooth, possibly metal or composite material, with a subtle sheen and no visible ventilation features. The overall design is practical, with a focus on durability and ease of access, typical of agricultural outbuildings found in rural or peri-urban settings.
description: An L-shaped cowshed with a dark, pitched roof, vertical wooden plank walls, multiple rectangular windows, and two solid doors. The structure is rustic and functional, designed for agricultural use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the L-shape, two main doors, and several windows on both wings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the longer wing with four windows and the intersection of the roof forms.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the uninterrupted expanse of the longer wing with five windows and no visible doors.
description_view_3: End view of the shorter wing, showing two windows and the gable end of the roof.
attribute_door_style: single solid door, likely wood or metal, with minimal design
attribute_window_presence: multiple rectangular windows, evenly spaced on both wings
